# Services & Dependencies

Every external service and library used in the 2k26 GM Grader Tool.

---

## Anthropic API

**What it is:** The AI backbone of the tool. All grading, reactions, summaries, and org consultations are generated by Claude.

**Endpoint:** `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages`  
**Model:** `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`  
**Auth:** API key passed in request header (never stored in the repo)

**How it's used:**

| Feature | Call type | Prompt style | Output format |
|---|---|---|---|
| Move analysis | Direct fetch | Structured JSON schema prompt | JSON |
| GM Summary | callClaude() helper | Plain instruction | Prose |
| Rumor Summary | callClaude() helper | Plain instruction | Prose |
| Consult Org | callClaude() helper | Delimiter format instructions | ###Tagged text |

**Key prompt engineering decisions:**
- Move analysis uses a strict grading rubric (most moves B/C, A reserved for elite decisions)
- Consult uses a Player Valuation Framework covering contract value, player tier, media perception, exec consensus, and recency weighting
- Consult output uses `###VOICE:` / `###QUOTE:` delimiters instead of JSON to avoid apostrophe/punctuation parse failures
- All prompts instruct Claude not to reference current real NBA GMs or teams by name (keeps the simulation clean)

**Docs:** [docs.anthropic.com](https://docs.anthropic.com)

---

## CDN Libraries

All loaded via `cdnjs.cloudflare.com` — no npm, no node_modules, no installation.

### React 18
**URL:** `cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/react/18.2.0/umd/react.production.min.js`  
**What it does:** Powers the entire UI — tabs, state management, conditional rendering, all component logic. Used in UMD mode (no JSX, no build step) via `React.createElement()` calls.

### ReactDOM 18
**URL:** `cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/react-dom/18.2.0/umd/react-dom.production.min.js`  
**What it does:** Renders the React component tree into the browser DOM via `ReactDOM.render()`.

### LZString 1.4.4
**URL:** `cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/lz-string/1.4.4/lz-string.min.js`  
**What it does:** Compresses the sync code payload. `moves[] + rumors[] + selectedTeam` as JSON compresses from ~15KB down to ~400 chars — short enough to paste in iMessage.  
**Method used:** `compressToEncodedURIComponent` / `decompressFromEncodedURIComponent`

### SheetJS (XLSX) 0.18.5
**URL:** `cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/xlsx/0.18.5/xlsx.full.min.js`  
**What it does:** Powers the Export → Excel feature. Converts the moves array into a formatted `.xlsx` file with 11 columns (date, move type, notes, grades, summaries, cap analysis, verdict) and triggers a browser download.

### QRCode.js 1.0.0
**URL:** `cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/qrcodejs/1.0.0/qrcode.min.js`  
**What it does:** Renders a scannable QR code in the Sync modal. The QR encodes the compact sync code so users can scan from their phone instead of typing. QR is generated client-side from the compressed sync string.

---

## Google Fonts

**URL:** `fonts.googleapis.com`  
**Fonts loaded:**
- **Barlow Condensed** (weights 600, 700, 800) — all headings, topbar, grade displays, power rankings
- **Barlow** (weights 400, 500, 600, 700) — body text, labels, buttons
- **IBM Plex Mono** (weights 400, 500) — monospace labels, timestamps, sync codes, grade pills

These three fonts are the entire visual language of the tool, modeled after NBA.com's design system.

---

## window.storage

**What it is:** Anthropic's artifact cloud storage API, available when the tool runs inside a Claude artifact context.  
**Scope:** Per artifact URL — resets when the artifact is re-published to a new URL.  
**Interface:** `window.storage.get(key)` / `window.storage.set(key, value)` / `window.storage.delete(key)`

**When it's not available** (e.g. opened as a standalone HTML file on GitHub Pages), the app degrades gracefully — all features work, but data only persists in React state for the current session. The sync code system handles cross-session portability in that case.

**Storage keys used:**
```
gmgrader-v5-moves     → JSON array of move objects
gmgrader-v5-rumors    → JSON array of rumor objects  
gmgrader-v5-gmsumm    → GM summary string
gmgrader-v5-rumsumm   → Rumor/situation summary string
gmgrader-v5-team      → Team ID string (e.g. "bulls")
```

---

## No Backend Services

To be explicit about what this tool does **not** use:
- No database (Postgres, Firebase, Supabase, etc.)
- No server (Node.js, Express, serverless functions, etc.)
- No auth (no accounts, no login)
- No analytics or tracking
- No third-party data APIs (NBA stats, player data, etc.)

All NBA GM names, team rosters, org voices, and power ranking scores are hardcoded in the HTML based on publicly known information as of early 2026.

---

## Environment Variables

The only secret in this project is the Anthropic API key, which is handled by the Claude artifact environment and never stored in the HTML file or this repository.

If you are forking this project and want to run it locally or deploy it yourself, you would need to either:
1. Add your own API key handling (a simple backend proxy or environment injection)
2. Or use the tool as-is through Claude.ai where the API key is managed by Anthropic

See `.env.example` in the root if one is provided.
